verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane-bound glycosyltransferase resident in the Golgi apparatus. The N-terminal hydrophobic signal sequence directs it to the Golgi, where it remains anchored via a retained signal sequence as a type II TM protein. The Golgi is an endomembrane compartment, not the plasma membrane. While type II TM proteins do have a luminal domain topologically analogous to an extracellular domain, that domain faces the Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. B4GALT1 has no documented plasma-membrane localization, PM-cycling pool, or cell-state-induced surface display. It is exclusively endomembrane-resident.
